chapter_summary: The gateway psalm — it contrasts the blessed person who loves and meditates on God's Word with the wicked who reject it. The blessed person is like a strong, fruit-bearing tree planted by a river; the wicked are like dry chaff the wind blows away. This psalm is an invitation to choose the blessed path.
main_lesson: A person who loves God's Word and follows it grows strong and fruitful, like a tree by a river.

memory_verse.kjv: Blessed is the man that walketh not in the counsel of the ungodly, nor standeth in the way of sinners, nor sitteth in the seat of the scornful.
memory_verse.little_bible: Happy is the person who doesn't take advice from people who don't love God, who doesn't hang around with those who do wrong, who doesn't sit and make fun of what is good.

parent_guide: Psalm 1 is the doorway to the whole Psalter — it sets out the two paths. Read it slowly, letting the tree image sink in. Ask your child: what does a tree need to grow? (water, roots, light) — then connect: God's Word is like that water and light. This is a wonderful psalm to memorise together as a family.
application_for_children: Every day you choose which path to walk. When you read God's Word, listen to it, and think about it — you are growing like that tree by the river.

verses.kjv: Blessed is the man that walketh not in the counsel of the ungodly, nor standeth in the way of sinners, nor sitteth in the seat of the scornful.
verses.little_bible: Oh, how happy is the person who doesn't follow the advice of those who ignore God, who doesn't join in when others do wrong, who doesn't sit with people who mock what is good and true!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Happy is the child who stays away from wrong and unkind things.
verses.meaning: True happiness begins with knowing what NOT to do — not following, joining, or sitting with those who reject God. Each step (walking, standing, sitting) describes how easily someone can slide deeper into the wrong crowd.
verses.memory_phrase: Happy is the one who chooses right.
verses.prayer: God, help me choose my friends and my steps wisely. I want to be truly happy — Your way.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between following bad advice and following good advice?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help each other in our family choose friends and habits that lead us toward God?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one good choice you can make today — and make it.
verses.action_challenge: Draw two paths — one that leads toward a bright, beautiful tree; one that leads away. Which path do you want to walk?
verses.illustration_prompt: A bright crossroads on a sunny day — one path leads toward a great green tree by a sparkling river; the other fades into grey dust. A small child stands at the crossroads, looking toward the tree. Warm, inviting light.

verses.kjv: But his delight is in the law of the LORD; and in his law doth he meditate day and night.
verses.little_bible: Instead, the happy person loves God's Word with all their heart — they think about it in the morning and at night, turning it over and over like a treasure they can't put down.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The happy person loves God's Word and thinks about it all day long.
verses.meaning: Delight means more than obedience — it means loving the Bible the way you love something wonderful. Meditating is like chewing — taking time to really savour and understand what God says.
verses.memory_phrase: I love and think about God's Word.
verses.prayer: God, make me love Your Word the way I love my favourite thing. Help me think about it day and night.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to 'delight' in something? What is something you truly delight in?
verses.family_discussion: How can we make reading and thinking about God's Word a joyful habit in our family?
verses.do_it_today: Pick one verse from the Bible today. Read it slowly. Think about it three times during the day.
verses.action_challenge: Write your favourite Bible verse on a card and put it somewhere you'll see it all day — maybe the fridge or your mirror.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child sitting cross-legged in golden morning light, an open Bible in their lap, smiling and thinking — a gentle thought-bubble shows the words glowing like treasure. Warm and peaceful.

verses.kjv: And he shall be like a tree planted by the rivers of water, that bringeth forth his fruit in his season; his leaf also shall not wither; and whatsoever he doeth shall prosper.
verses.little_bible: That person will be like a beautiful tree growing right beside a river — always drinking in water, always green, always bearing fruit at the right time. Whatever they do will grow and flourish.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They will be like a big strong tree by the river, always green, always growing good fruit.
verses.meaning: The tree is not just surviving — it is thriving. Planted by rivers means constantly fed. Fruit in season means the right things at the right time. This is the picture of a life rooted in God's Word.
verses.memory_phrase: I am like a tree planted by the river.
verses.prayer: God, make me like that tree — rooted in You, always drinking from Your Word, growing fruit that helps others.
verses.discussion_question: Why does a tree planted near a river grow better than one planted far from water?
verses.family_discussion: What are some 'fruits' — good things — that grow in a family who stays close to God?
verses.do_it_today: Go outside and look at a tree. Think: my roots in God's Word help me grow strong like this tree.
verses.action_challenge: Draw or paint your own tree by a river. Label the roots with things from God's Word that make you strong.
verses.illustration_prompt: A magnificent, lush tree with deep roots stretching into a clear river, its branches full of green leaves and bright fruit. Golden warm light. The tree is full of life and completely healthy.

verses.kjv: The ungodly are not so: but are like the chaff which the wind driveth away.
verses.little_bible: But those who turn away from God are completely different — they are not strong or rooted. They are like the dry, empty husks of wheat that the wind just blows away.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: But people who ignore God are like dry bits the wind blows away — not rooted at all.
verses.meaning: Chaff is the worthless husk left after wheat is threshed — it has no weight, no root, no staying power. One gust of wind and it is gone. This is a sobering but loving warning: without God's Word, nothing lasts.
verses.memory_phrase: Without God, we are like chaff in the wind.
verses.prayer: God, I don't want to be empty chaff. Keep me rooted in You so I am not blown away.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever blown on something light and watched it fly away? Why is being like chaff not a good thing?
verses.family_discussion: What are the 'roots' our family has in God that keep us steady when hard winds come?
verses.do_it_today: Blow on a feather or a dandelion. Think: this is what a life without God is like — no roots. Then thank God for being your roots.
verses.action_challenge: Compare a dry leaf (chaff) and a green growing leaf. Which one do you want to be? Tell God why.
verses.illustration_prompt: A swirl of dry, golden-grey chaff being swept away by wind against a pale sky — lightweight, rootless, scattered. Contrast with a glimpse of the strong rooted tree from the previous verse. The emptiness of the chaff is clear.

verses.kjv: Therefore the ungodly shall not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous.
verses.little_bible: So when God's great day of judgement comes, those who have refused Him will not be able to stand — they will have nothing to hold them up. They will not belong among those who walked with God.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Those who reject God will not stand on judgement day — they have no roots to hold them.
verses.meaning: The image of 'standing' in judgement is powerful — the chaff cannot stand before a holy God. This is not a cruel verdict but a logical one: those who chose the path away from God arrive at the destination of that path.
verses.memory_phrase: God's way leads to standing firm.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that because of Jesus, I can stand before You. Help me walk Your path.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to 'stand' before someone — to be ready and not ashamed?
verses.family_discussion: God is just and good. How does knowing there is a judgement day help us choose to do right every day?
verses.do_it_today: Stand up tall. Tell God: 'I want to stand firm in You — help me choose Your way today.'
verses.action_challenge: Talk about what makes us able to 'stand' before God. (Hint: it's not our own goodness — it's trusting in Jesus.)
verses.illustration_prompt: A great, luminous, open space — like a courtroom bathed in holy white light. Wispy shapes of chaff drift and dissolve in the light while a rooted, strong figure stands firm. Awe-inspiring and reverent.

verses.kjv: For the LORD knoweth the way of the righteous: but the way of the ungodly shall perish.
verses.little_bible: God knows every step of the path of those who love Him — He watches over it, He walks it with them. But the path that turns away from God leads to an end that has no future.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God knows and watches over the path of those who love Him. The wrong path leads nowhere good.
verses.meaning: 'Knoweth' here means more than knowing about — it means God is intimately acquainted with, watching over, caring for. The righteous path is personally shepherded by God. The other path is abandoned.
verses.memory_phrase: God knows my path and walks with me.
verses.prayer: Thank You, God, that You know my path and walk with me. I choose Your way.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between someone knowing your name and someone truly knowing you?
verses.family_discussion: God 'knows' the way of the righteous — He is personally with us on this path. How does that make you feel?
verses.do_it_today: Every time you take a step today, remember: God knows this path. He is walking it with me.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, say together: 'God knows our path. He walks with us.' Then pray and choose to walk His way this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: A golden sunlit path stretching into a bright, beautiful horizon — a figure walking it accompanied by a warm, gentle glow beside them (the presence of God). In the background, a grey, fading path disappears into shadow. The contrast between life and loss is clear but not dark.
